message.content: Adversaries may use System Language Discovery to infer the geographical location of a victim's system, shaping their subsequent actions to avoid detection and prosecution. This technique involves querying system defaults, keyboard layouts, and environment variables to determine the system language. Common methods include querying the Windows Registry, using Windows API functions like GetUserDefaultUILanguage and GetKeyboardLayoutList, and checking environment variables on macOS and Linux systems. Malware such as Avaddon, Bazar, Clop, and REvil use these checks to avoid infecting systems in specific regions, particularly those in the Commonwealth of Independent States (CIS). Mitigation strategies include monitoring for unusual registry queries and API calls related to language settings, implementing endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to detect and block suspicious behaviors, and employing network segmentation to limit the spread of malware. Regularly updating and patching systems can also reduce the risk of exploitation.
